summary: The US-Iran conflict remains the dominant market force this week, as Iran has effectively halted Strait of Hormuz shipping since late February and Brent crude surged above $103 following Trump's blockade order. Stocks and bonds are recovering on renewed hopes for a deal after Iranian officials reached out to the Trump administration, lifting sentiment broadly.
tweet: Iran war ceasefire hopes are driving this week's cross-asset moves — stocks and bonds rebound while gold stays bid and crypto lags as the Fed stays on hold. --- SUMMARY: The US-Iran conflict remains the dominant market...
